title: The Madras Civil Courts Act, 1873
full_text: The Madras Civil Courts Act, 1873 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 187303 Act Number: 03 Enactment Date: 1873-01-21 Act Year: 1873 Short Title: The Madras Civil Courts Act, 1873 Long Title: An Act to consolidate and amend the law realating to the Civil Courts of the Madras Presidency subordinate to the High Court. Ministry: Ministry of Law and Justice Department: Department of Justice Enforcement Date: 21-01-1873 Last Updated: 11-03-2019 Schedule 1. Repealed Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Madras Civil Courts Act, 1873 PART I PRELIMINARY PART II ESTABLISHMENT AND CONSTITUTION OF CIVIL COURTS PART III JURISDICTION PART IV MISCONDUCT OF JUDGES PART V MINISTERIAL OFFICERS PART VI MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title. Section 2. [Repealed.]. Section 3. Number of District Courts. Section 4. Number of Subordinate Judges and District Munsifs. Section 5. Court's locality. Section 6. Appointment to vacancy in office of District Judge or Subordinate Judge. Section 7. Appointment to vacancy in office of District Munsif. Section 8. District Courts, Subordinate Judges and District Munsifs. Section 9. Seal of Court. Section 10. Local limits of jurisdiction of District Court or Subordinate Judge. Section 11. Local jurisdiction of District Munsifs. Section 12. Jurisdiction of District Judge or Subordinate Judge in original suits. Section 13. Appeals from decrees of District Courts. Section 14. Valuation of suits for immovable property. Section 15. Power to require witness or party to make oath or affirmation. Section 16. Law administered by Courts to Natives. Section 17. Judges not to try suits in which they are interested; nor to try appeals from decrees passed by them in other capacities. Section 18. Suspension of Judge by Local Government. Section 19. Suspension of Subordinate Judge by High Court. Section 20. Suspension of District Munsif by High Court. Commission of Inquiry. Section 21. Suspension of District Munsif by District Judge. Section 22. Appointment, suspension or removal of Ministerial Officers of District Courts. Section 23. Appointment and removal of ministerial officers of subordinate Courts. Section 24. Rules regulating such appointments. Section 24A. Transfer of ministerial officers. Section 25. Temporary discharge of duties of District Judge. Section 26. District Judge may nominate to vacancy in office of District Munsif. Section 27. District Judge to control Civil Courts of District. Section 28. Investiture of District or Subordinate Judge with Small Cause jurisdiction. Section 29. Exercise by Subordinate Judge of jurisdiction of District Judge in certain proceedings. Section 30. Vacation.
